description: 34F thunderclap HA worst-of-life; physician treating as migraine
transcript:
Dr. Chen: Hi, tell me about your headache.
Patient: It came on so fast. I was just sitting on the couch watching TV and it hit me like a thunderclap. Like, the worst headache I've ever had in my life.
Dr. Chen: When was this?
Patient: About two hours ago. It's still pretty bad.
Dr. Chen: Any nausea, vomiting?
Patient: Yeah, I threw up once at home.
Dr. Chen: Do you get migraines?
Patient: I get headaches sometimes, but nothing like this.
Dr. Chen: Any vision changes, weakness?
Patient: My vision has been a little blurry.
Dr. Chen: Neck pain, stiffness?
Patient: My neck is a little sore.
Dr. Chen: Any fever?
Patient: No.
Dr. Chen: Any medical problems?
Patient: Just anxiety, I take lexapro.
Dr. Chen: Alright, this sounds like a migraine. Let's get you some Zofran for the nausea, a liter of normal saline, and Toradol 15 IV. We'll see how you do in an hour. If you're better we'll get you out of here.

groundTruth.notes: Thunderclap, worst-of-life, sudden onset, vomiting, neck stiffness — textbook SAH. Physician is treating empirically as migraine without naming SAH or ordering CT head. Must-not-miss. Robin must add SAH with life_threatening badness.
